title: Key Derivation, HKDF, and Key Separation · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for key derivation, hkdf, and key separation and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit native, entropy, interoperability, side-channel, compliance, or production transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for labeled key schedule and derivation tree. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish extraction, expansion, password stretching, entropy concentration limits, and deterministic key schedules.
Apply HKDF extract and expand with supported hashes, salt, labeled info, bounded output, and one-way key schedule ownership.
Derive independent role, direction, epoch, algorithm, and purpose keys from a versioned hierarchy without key cycles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A quiz cryptographic engineering team handles case 1356 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive bounded purpose- and role-separated HKDF keys bound to the negotiated transcript; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish extraction, expansion, password stretching, entropy concentration limits, and deterministic key schedules.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Version derivation labels and parameters, preserve old read paths, prevent downgrade, and prove rotation rollback.

hints: For Cryptography and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Crypto Go Kdf Version Migration, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Crypto Go Kdf Version Migration precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Cryptography and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Crypto Go Kdf Version Migration, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz cryptographic engineering team handles case 1675 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive bounded purpose- and role-separated HKDF keys bound to the negotiated transcript; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
